question:
A cross-over trial sought to learn whether oat bran cereal lowered low-density lipoprotein (LDL) cholesterol in hypercholesterolemic men. Twelve subjects completed the study. Half were randomly assigned a diet that included oat bran cereal. The other half were assigned a diet that included corn flakes. Dietary interventions were applied for 2 weeks, after which LDL levels $(\mathrm{mmol} / \mathrm{L})$ were recorded. Subjects were then crossed-over to the alternative diet for 2 weeks. LDL was once again measured. Table 11.1 lists data from the study.

Paired samples are analyzed by creating a new variable to hold within pair differences. Call this new variable DELTA. Table 11.1 lists DELTA val-ues created by subtracting each oat bran value from each corn flake value for each individual. Positive DELTA values reflect lower LDL on the oat bran diet.
